reasoning: The S&P 500 experienced a >5% drop on April 4, 2025, driven by Trump tariff fears. Between April 5 and June 8, no subsequent drops of this magnitude occurred; instead, the index saw strong monthly gains in May. Market volatility peaked with the VIX hitting ~60 in April but appears to be normalizing. Given the passage of time without a repeat event, the stabilization of the current trend, and the base rate of multiple isolated >5% drops in non-gfc/covid years, a slight downward adjustment from the late-May Manifold prediction of 43% to 38% is warranted.
